sentences.text: Oh.
Hey, Mary Kite.
Hey, how's it going?
Doing real well, how have you been?
I've been good, it's been a while.
Yeah, how's, like… I was looking at her notes, like, a little over a year, so how's… anything, crazy happen in between now and then?
Oh, you know, just the regular day-to-day and property management.
So yeah, pretty much craziness all the time, then.
All the time, but it's good.
Keeps us busy.
Nice.
Are you… are you still in Dallas, or…
Yes, yep, we're still in Dallas.
We recently moved offices, but we… We've got a bit of an upgrade on our office space.
Okay.
Are we… are we talking, like, ping pong tables and, like, a…
Oh, yeah.
Okay, no.
Ping pong now.
Got a massage room.
That's awesome.
Massage room is… that's… that's a first, I haven't heard of that.
That's nice.
It's a massage chair, so…
Okay.
Yeah, pretty nice.
Yeah, it's been… it's been a good move.
I'd spend some time in there.
Yeah, definitely.
Me.

Well, that's great to hear, and I don't know how much Todd had filled you in, but, Justin and myself had been working with him for probably, like, the last, like, 6 months or so, in the client services team.
Actually, here comes Todd.
But, excited to hear that it sounds like there's some excitement now on the ops side, as far as, with ApartmentIQ.
Yeah, definitely.
He did fill me in a little bit, earlier today.
So yes, happy to open the conversation back up and get to see what's changed in the past year, if anything, and just kind of get a refresh.
Yeah.
Pick them up.

Yeah, I saw Todd briefly came in here, and it popped out, so maybe he'll come back, but yeah, he was telling me it… you guys were kind of, like, on pause for a while, but now there's, like, a strong preference, is what he was saying, to… like, are you guys… were you guys still just… you stayed status quo, kind of just manually filling out the spreadsheets?
Is that, like, what you guys have been doing?
Yes, yep, we've been… still doing that.
Okay.
So yes, we're excited.
I think there's just been a big push of getting something that is automated and can just be done quickly.
Sure, yeah, and as you can recall, that's what we're all about here.
So, on our end, we've had some major changes since you last seen us, so we're really excited to show you that.
Awesome.

Todd mentioned, like, maybe there were some concerns with just, like, concessions in the past, I guess, as we're going through this, like, anything that, like, you knew in the past that was kind of, like, causing you guys some hesitation, that I could help, you know, maybe point out that we've made a change, or did anything stick out to you in the past?
Not that… not that I can recall off the top of my head.
When we previously looked.
a year ago, we were looking at a few different companies, so it kind of all is blending together.
So I'm trying to pull up my notes, but if we want to just get started, I'll let you know if anything comes up to concern, but I'm excited to see what What all's changed?
Yeah, absolutely, I'll bring it up here.
Hey, Todd, can you hear us?
Yes, sir, can you hear me okay?
I can.
How you been?
Hey, I've been doing good, man, thank you.
Great, I was just catching up with Mary-Kate here, it sounds… she was telling us you guys got the new massage room, too, at the… at the new office.
Yeah, yeah, we do.
Yeah, it's, it's a lot nicer now.
Yeah, it sounds like it.
I'm definitely jealous.
Hopefully, hopefully, if we kick off one of these trials, I'll get down to, Dallas and get to see it in person.
So, definitely, excited to kind of see what the new office looks like.
Yeah, absolutely.
Well, yeah, I appreciate y'all jumping on.
This comes at a good time, and I apologize for being late.
No, all good.
I was just kind of catching up with Mary Kate, was gonna, you know, show the differences since the last time she saw it.

Obviously, you're getting a pretty almost expert level at this point, Todd, but, of seeing the new… application, but, happy to show the changes that we've had just with the layout, a few new floor plan information, and also updates to our concessions.
So, without further ado here, Mary Kay, can you see the screen now?
Yes, I can.
Cool.
So, when you come on in, you'll notice just the layout might look just a little more clean and easier to understand as the constant feedback that we get from our teams.
So, obviously, this is something that your site teams to regionals, to RVPs, executives would be able to easily just operate.
So, just kind of back, all your owned and managed properties would all be located right here under the My Properties.
Let me just… just kicked me out, of course.
But when you're in there, I'll just go into 1200 Broadway for an example.
You'll see your property will be the highlighted property.
concession details, so you'll be able to see the concession percentage, as well as what's being offered at the property.
Our AI technology for picking up the concessions is second to none in the industry.
So whether it's a pop-up, a banner, or a baked-in image, you'll be able to see it.
And it's even smart enough to… so, like, here's a good example to even differentiate between different types of concessions.
So you're able to quickly see, like, hey, this is just a two months free for the studios, and then we have the two and a half months free for the ones, twos, and threes.
So we'll make all those calculations for you, and point it out for you.
I think you'll notice we also have Google reviews, it's probably something that's new since the last time you saw us.
You'll notice then all your calculated metrics.
So again, something that's really exclusive, to ApartmentIQ is we'll even calculate an advertise occupancy for you.
So I know you looked at a few other providers before.
Something that's really unique to ApartmentIQ is our performance metrics.
So, because we're going to the actual property website, we're able to expose all the available units.
So you're able to go into the floor plan, and then even go down to the unit level.
So now we have all these pop-up boxes.

See, for instance, if you wanted to see, you know, what are the 70 vacant units at 1111 Church, you can just click over that metric and pop open a really quick pop-up box here and see, you know, what are the units, what's the floor plan type.
Did it just get leased?
And then what's the concession value?
I'll kind of pause here for the… I know this is a little bit newer layout, Anything on here worth diving into more?
Do y'all count it as a leash just when the, when they take it off of their website?
Yeah, good question.
So, what we'll do is, as soon as it comes off the website, because we're updating every single day, we'll account it as an application.
And then if it stays offline for 7 days, then it's turned into a lease.
So it's another… again, I can't emphasize enough just how much data quality control that we have in place now.
You're really just getting the most up-to-date concessions, unit-level availability.
We're not going to Apartments.
com.
I know, like, other providers that try to do a similar… a lot of their information is pulled from ILS, in particular Apartments.
com.
Which has a lot of caps on, like, if you go to Apartments.
com, you know, a lot of the… a lot of the listings will just say, you know, there's, you know, 5 to 8 units of every floor plan available.
With us, because we're going to that leasing portal section, that's how you're able to actually see all of, you know, we're tracking, you know, 30, 70 plus units available at these properties.
Okay.
How do y'all account for that for the leasing portals that do have caps, too?
Is there a way for your system to tell?
Like, some of our properties, we only… if we have 15, available units in one floor plan, we're only going to market 6 of them online.
On our website, so is there something to flag?
Yeah, there's two… so two-fold to that.
One, basically, the system is sophisticated enough to go around that classic, you know, driving marketing urgency.
What we do is, it's a series of moving… move-in dates and filters, and amenities, so it will actually just run all the unit numbers, and if you put plug in those unit numbers on that leasing portal.
It will show an available unit, even if it's hidden.
But also, we will, to your point, too, we also will flag, particularly more in the case for, like, a lease-up, if there's, you know.
a portion of the units that have never even reached, the market.

We'll actually have a little limited data tag as well, so, you're able to actually also get a heads up on it, but, to answer your question, it's a… yes, there is a way around that, but then, two, we also will flag it for you, so you also just, you know, can take it with a grain of salt.
Okay.
And then, hey, Brian, I don't see you join in here, I don't think we've met before.
Hey, how's it going?
Not too bad, it's nice to meet you.
We're just kind of going through the first flow here of the market survey.
As you go down, too, we've added a lot more of fees and deposits coverage, so you're able to have fees and deposits, and then in-unit and community-level amenities.

Again, all of our, if you'll recall, all of our reports, too, are exportable and automated, so you can come in here, PDF or Excel, and simply put in the email address that you want sent to, you want it sent, you know, on every Monday you want it hitting your inbox, you just go ahead and schedule that, and it will go right into your inbox.
So I'll be able… I can send over some, examples of those PDFs, but just really clean, nice to easy-understand PDFs just right… right in there.
This floor plan… report is a brand new, since you would have seen, ApartmentIQ.
This will also incorporate, if you recall, we have the most historical data, that's publicly available.
So we have, actually, starting next month, so back to October of 2020, we'll have 5 years of historical data.
So some other companies maybe have maybe, like, a year and a half or so.
We'll have 5 years of coverage.
And you'll be able to see any time frame, so I just select the last 30 days.
And you'll be able to see exactly how your property's been performing.
I guess, Brian, fill me in a little bit, I guess, for your role, just so I can maybe show you a few features that would be easy for you.
Brian's on Todd's team, so he does a lot of the, budgeting for new clients.
Okay.
Yes.
Are we okay?
Your mic's going in and out, Todd.
Yeah.
Quick.
Heavenly.
Brian might have stepped in your dog's office in Washington.
Okay.
Yeah, I guess just in the meantime, Mary-Kate, it… how does this… this layout look… look to you?
I mean, it looks clean, it looks easy to read, I remember liking y'all's UI last time as well, when we first met, so this is look… it looks… Similar, but a little different to them.
Yeah, so we added this to just incorporate the rent trends on the right.
with current performance.

So, you can easily see, you know, like, price per square foot-wise, you can filter this high to low, exactly where you rank, you know, by studios, by ones, by twos, and you can also see, you know, have you been decreasing rent?
Or, like, this is a great example, they've really had to decrease their studios, but over the last 30 days, they've been able to increase their ones by You know, almost 32% here.
Hey guys, sorry about that, can y'all hear me now?
Yeah.
Alright, sorry, yeah, Brian's computer froze, and my mic was fading in and out, so…
the real… it's a real situation over here.
Yeah.
So, yeah, Brian's on the client services team, so he's helping underwrite, kind of with what we were looking at originally, Austin.
Yeah, and then once MK's got what she needs on a tutorial side, or kind of an update side, MK, you can let me know, and I can take it from the next step.
Okay.
Perfect.
So yeah, this is just really easy to understand, and then we even get a little bit more granular.
You can even see the different unit types performance by floor plan.
So now you can see, you know, are the large studios.
you can filter that high to low and see, like, are the large studios doing better than the smaller studios, for instance?
All this is really quick and easy to understand.
You can see who the lease leader is, you know, 805 Lee here, they've leased 4, of their S1, for an example.
All this can be filtered to specific metrics that are important to you, or if you just wanted to see specific properties.
We've also added this historical piece.

So, really leverages that 5 years of historicals that we have, and you can see how your property's been performing rent-wise, so you'll be the purple line in here versus the median, and the min and the max.
You're able to see how you've been changing your rent rates month over month versus your competitors.
concession values.
I know a lot of our third-party managers really like to also use, this table right here.
So we're now showing you leased… number of leases that you have by property by month.
to being able to just come back to ownership and, you know, confidently tell them, hey, it was a slow month, but we still were, you know, the lease leader versus our cons.
Or, you know, we just deployed a new marketing spend here in February, and then the next two months, you know, you had 21 and 13 leases.
So a lot of different, like.
Stories that you can actually tell with some, solid data to back it up.
Anything… Is it kind of coming back to you?
Yes, yeah, it is.
Okay.
Can you remind me what the implementation process looks like?
Yeah, it's… it's literally as fast as you want to go.
So, because we don't require any integrations, it's just giving us your, property list with just name and address and unit count, and we get it set up for you.

So, obviously, an account of your guys' size, too, we have, if you recall, we have a team of over 100, so you guys would also get a dedicated customer success team, a dedicated training team, so really, I mean, like, we've… you know, got 200 unit… 200,000 unit portfolios live, you know, within a month.
Basically, as fast as you want to go.
Okay, awesome.
And is there any limit on the number of users?

No, and I relayed this, I know Todd and I have been working with Corey on our team on pricing, like, you guys would get unlimited users, so, you know, portfolio of your size would have 100-plus users in here easily.
It's easy to add and remove, so right here in the user management, you just would have the manage users, you would invite a new user.
Again, this is something that your customer success team can handle, or we also like to just make it easy for you.
Simply put in the email address and select their role.
So, like, you generally, for instance, your community managers and leasing agents would be, like, editor and read-only, and they would have access to just their property, right?
So, when they can't come in, it would just be nice and easy for them to just know their property.
Training, too, is, we now also have, in addition to, like, you'll get live training when you initially onboard, but we also have, live training every week.
So, it's really nice for when you get new hires, or you get new folks just wanting to, you know, sharpen up on their skills, they can always schedule a real live training.
It's not a, you know, I know some of our others some of the other folks out there, it's kind of like, you know, here's a YouTube video.
You can also get, live training, too.
That sounds good.
I think that's all the questions I had on my end, Todd.
I want to leave some time for you and Brian as well.
Cool.
Yeah, thanks, MK.
So, guys, I appreciate y'all's time.
I'll kind of give you, kind of where we are, kind of coming from the sales side myself, I'll be very transparent.
So, when we first started talking, it was, hey.
This is gonna be for my team, these markets, with the potential for maybe rolling out the rest of the portfolio.
Now MK's, Mary Kate is involved as well.
We're looking to roll this out, immediately across.
Our platform, with, with one company, versus both of our sides using different companies.
The caveats to what's changing is to, a couple different models that we've gotta… that we've gotta solve for, for you guys to look at on the pricing side.

And that we're doing with the various groups that we're looking at, today and tomorrow, kind of giving everybody the same, like, hey, here's what we need for where we're going, and see what's possible.
But ultimately, it would be an immediate rollout.

of this, with the mark… so, scenario one, the markets we provided you before, but also we need API access for… to be able to feed in the rental data, the rents, into, into our system, kind of like the ownership data, so that's really the only… kind of, I guess, material change from what we discussed before, is what does it look like to include, the calls to grab the… the rent data and occupancy data and so forth into… into our system, for those markets.
I think I could be, and it would just be for, like, the, you know.
And really, it would be… at this time, the only data that we would be live-feeding rents with would be Dallas, Fort Worth.
Houston, Austin, San Antonio.
Denver… Tampa and Orlando.
The rest of those markets, we don't actually have activity in currently, so we wouldn't be live feeding the rent side, that would just be ownership data.

So there's that component, and then there's a second, pricing of having the same rent data feeding into… the, into BI, or, you know, the APIs, but getting national, market ownership data, and be able to go and look at rent data for the rest of the country.
But just not necessarily the live feed of the rents themselves for the markets we're not operating in.
And so that would be the second pricing scenario.

So what we're gonna solve for is… a live feed, you know, the open APIs to be able to pull in data and ownership data for the markets that we operate in, and then a national side as well, so that we can see what's going on in the rest of the nation.
And so they're basically the two pricing sides.
Do we go all national now?
Or, and what does that look like?
Or just do it to the markets we have.

And I've been given the direction to… To figure that out, and there's no committee to go to, it's… Like, how do we solve for this, and what does the pricing become, and who can get it the best price, so that we can get our clients to sign off, and… And execute a contract as quickly as possible.
Hey, Todd, for, for, the properties you're looking for ownership group information on, is it every vintage?
Does unit size matter, I guess, class, I guess?
What's kind of the scope there?
Yeah, like, 150 units and up is really the focus, kind of 1980s and newer, but, yeah, 150 units and up is really the biggest caveat to that.
Anything smaller isn't necessarily relevant.
Okay.
For what we're, we're doing on the ownership.
Tracking side.
That's awful.
Okay, awesome, yeah.
Yeah, so it's, you know… We… we kind of have two different businesses, so you guys, obviously, talking to us as Valiant.
We have the management company that operates in those patch 12 markets we gave you, and then we have our capital business.
That operates nationally.
And so, we spoke very highly of your product, and a couple others, and kind of said, hey, let's… let's look at kind of consolidating these platforms.
Or maybe just an add-on, but maybe go national.
So that was, you know, what we like here is the ability to utilize our built-in platform.
Using your open APIs and the calls.
To… to be able to leverage our platform even further.
But yeah, so that's kind of where this came up, is looking at it for the 12 markets we're in, and NASH.
Okay, yeah, that definitely helps.
You know, I think we'll go back, and I'd have to imagine, kind of like we mentioned earlier, I'd have to imagine we'd make it pretty simple and just give you an all-in price.
Yeah.
And it's… And it, again, as it would be for the 30,000… like, I think you said, like, 30,000 plus units on the management side, and then just the markets that we've already been discussing.
That's right, yeah, we have 37,000 units right now, and the intent is to try to onboard another 3,000 before the year's over.
With the intent of being 50,000 by the end of next year.
Okay.
So, and then there's just some other things in the background that may, you know, could cause us to get larger, too.
But, yeah, looking at it as, these are the markets we operate in, so we need the live feed of the rent data into our system.

And the ownership data, but then having… so that being option one, and then option two is still getting the same data beating into with the rents and the ownership data, but now having national, you know, being able to log into ApartmentIQ and get the national You know, go to any market.
Right.
No, that makes total sense.

And then, kind of just as far… it sounds like now, too, like, just, I know when I was talking with Corey, you know, we'd align on when, like, your contract end dates and all that, so you're not kind of doubling up, but it sounds like, you know, sooner than later is when you'd like to get this done.
Yeah, so that… that's a great segue, great.
So we need to start… we… we want access as soon as possible.
But we can't pay until January because of the budget season, and getting all the clients that are signing off on it.
Right.
So, figuring out how can we get access Start it, and just post-bill us for that, so that we can, start to integrate your system into our end-of-year, strategic planning for 2026.
But because of the budget year and the way the operating works at the property level, we wouldn't be able to pay the invoices until, you know, starting in January.
Sure.
No, I pre… and, you know, that would be no issue.
I can guarantee that, so, Appreciate that insight.
Well, great.
Anything else?
Did you want me to show… is Brian still there to show the Explore feature, or anything else, Todd, that is at the top of your mind?
No, I think we're good right now.
His computer was freezing up on him.
Okay.
So I think, you know, really, it kind of comes down to.
what is that gonna look like?
Because I think, before it was some of the operational, you know, things that I mentioned before, and MK was asking me questions about today, but more so, I think the cost of the API side.
was just really costly, comparatively, for that.
And so, it just kind of… at the end of the day.
every system has really good things, and things you wish were different, and, you know, kind of like a CRM and everything else.
So it's really gonna come down to, like, who can get us the best access and data that feeds into our system, is basically what I was given to by my partner, and and how quickly we can then onboard.
So, he, he actually walked in my office right before we got on this call and said.
Time is of the essence.
just… let's just get this done.

So, they're just kind of having you guys… getting y'all that information so you can take it back and see how those two pricing scenarios would work, including the… the APIs for the rent data and stuff, will then give us what we need, and we'll be execute… making a quick decision, across these.
What's cool, too, I was gonna say, just in terms of, like, speed of getting set up, I mean, our team builds, like, the market surveys for, like, your actual portfolio.
We build these things really quick.
So, like, realistically, like.
the two of you could have access to the platform in, like, one or two business weeks.
The lag time, which could maybe take, like, a month, would actually just be the trainings, like, coordinating those.
So just keep that in mind, and we could have your actual market surveys set up in a week or two.
Yeah, I think the big thing would be just talking to y'all's BI team, or however all that works, whoever that would be to connect with our BI people.
to talk about the integration, would be a big piece of it as we go through the pricing.
But, yeah, I think… what do you think as far as a timeline to get pricing?
I mean, I…
That's what we threw out at you.
Yeah, I can get… I mean, I'll get with Corey immediately after this call, so, I mean, pricing… you would expect it early next week at the latest.
I mean, I know we would get something very quick.
Cool.
Sounds good.
I appreciate it.
Yeah, we, in full transparency, we're going back-to-back-to-back on these for the next 2 hours.
Okay.

So, with, with basically every group that you guys know, where it's like, they said, let's do it, we've played around with them all, like… Let's make some decisions on who's gonna be our strategic partner for the long term, and and, you know, make the decision and just move, and not… not keep kind of playing around with it, so… We're serious and ready to get things moving.
It's just a dollars and cents game, and, you know, we'd love to work with you guys.
We'd love a long-term partnership.
Yeah.

Yeah, likewise, and it sounds like, too, I think it would help, I know you mentioned with some of your other contracts, like, it sounds like even maybe, like, a multi-year option, we could get aggressive with pricing there, too.
Would you be open to that?
Yeah, I think so, certainly.
You know, for… If we're getting in together, you know, especially maybe it's the first 12 months, and then it's a long, you know, time, like, if we're gonna spend the money on our end to build out.
the connectivity to your system from the API standpoint, and do all of that, like, we definitely aren't looking for a short-term relationship.
Right.

But we do want to make sure what we're doing is working and going to work, so I could see it being a little bit shorter, for the first, and then doing something, you know, longer thereafter, because, you know, it's not worth us spending a ton of money, you know, to just switch all the time.

But we do want to make sure, like, you're starting to open up APIs and do a bunch of different things, like, just want to make sure it all, you know, we can make it all work the way everyone desires to.
That makes total sense.
So yeah, maybe 12 months to begin, and then maybe a 2-year option, but then once that gets up, then a long-term one.
Yeah.
Okay, and then lastly, I might have had it in my notes, but, like, you mentioned, like, your BI team.
I guess, what… are you using, like, Power BI, or Domo, or what's the data warehouse that you're using?
So we use Power BI, and then we have AWS for, like, some of our data storage, and then we have our own internal servers as well, but Power BI is ultimately what drives the, I guess, the outputs.
Okay, that's, that's what I thought, but, well, that definitely helps.
Well, great.
Anything… anything else?
Other than that, I definitely have, some work to do.

I'll go to bat on some pricing for y'all, and would it… would it be, like, it doesn't sound like even much of a… like, even a trial period, like, it would just be kind of a pricing decision, and then get you guys going?
Yeah, I mean, I think there could end up being, like, a 2-week or 3-week trial period, but I doubt it.

Like, I mean, I think it's… they're in a spot where, as an executive team, we want to pull the trigger, so when pricing comes out, if it turns into, you know… Where the pricing ties back to the same of two groups and so forth, and there may be something where we test out a couple and solicit some feedback.
But our intent is that, I'll be honest, my intent is, I hope you guys are able to get a really aggressive, good pricing for what we're trying to accomplish, and we just roll… roll with you guys.
And we don't end up in that, but, yeah, that… That could potentially occur, but that's not our hope at this point.
Okay, yeah, I appreciate that, and yeah, we're real hopeful for a partnership, too, and I guess, will you be around the office, like, the next couple weeks, too?
I will be.
Okay.
I know we're planning a trip down to Dallas to see a few others, here this month, so definitely would like to, to meet up in person if possible, too.
Yeah.
Yeah, no, that sounds great.
Yeah, we'll look forward to hearing from you guys soon, and if you have any questions, just let me know.
I'll be offline a little bit tomorrow and Monday, so if you need something or have a question on clarifying something, you can reach me on my cell phone, and we can get you clarity so that you need.

Okay, well, yeah, appreciate that, and then it sounds like, too, I'll also… I'll get the pricing, and then, also, we actually just, there's a new hire that's specifically here to work with, like, API feeds as well, so, sounds like getting them introduced with your BI team would be great.
Awesome, yeah, that sounds great.
Well, thank y'all for the time, we look forward to hearing from you soon.
Likewise, Todd, really appreciate it.
Yeah, thanks, y'all take care.
Thanks a lot.
